page: Amazon Web Services r6in.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1892.68 and a multi-core score of 30929.24. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1658.65 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds reach 139.8 GB/sec and 66.88 GB/sec respectively. In database operations, the instance processes 30.91 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 14854.17 on PassMark database operations.

The instance supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration flags like AES and SHA_NI. For language model inference, it demonstrates strong performance in text generation with a small 135M model at 298.34 tokens/sec, though performance drops to a weak 5.91 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large 70B models. Qualitatively, the r6in.8xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive workloads that require high network throughput but do not need local NVMe storage. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across compute and memory metrics, with a PassMark CPU score of 36693.52 and a Redis SET throughput of 30.91 million operations per second. It also exhibits strong text generation speeds of 298.34 tokens/sec for small language models. This hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy enterprise workloads, database operations, and high-throughput network applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C at 3.5 GHz
256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz with 8.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
No local storage or GPU, optimized for network-attached EBS storage
Strong text generation performance of 298.34 tokens/sec on small LLM models
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
